# xelon_object_storage_user (Resource)

The object storage user resource allows you to manage Xelon Object Storage users.

Users are used to create access keys and manage access to Object Storage buckets.

-> The Xelon API automatically creates a default access key when an Object Storage user is created.
To ensure access credentials are managed explicitly through Terraform, the provider removes the
default access key during creation of the `xelon_object_storage_user` resource.
Use the `xelon_object_storage_access_key` resource to create and manage Object Storage credentials.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "xelon_object_storage_user" "test" {
  name          = "testuser"
  region        = "zh1"
  storage_limit = 500
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the object storage user.
- `region` (String) The region of the object storage user.
- `storage_limit` (Number) The storage limit of the object storage user in GB.

### Optional

- `tenant_id` (String) The ID of the tenant that owns the object storage user.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the object storage user.
- `s3_endpoints` (Set of String) The set of S3-compatible endpoint URLs that can be used to access object storage.
- `zone_replication_enabled` (Boolean) Whether zone replication is enabled for the object storage user.
